steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Collect all $x$ terms: $x(r - 7) - 19x = 22$, so $x(r - 26) = 22$. A linear equation $ax = b$ has exactly one solution when $a ≠ 0$. The question asks which value of $r$ does NOT give exactly one solution — meaning $r - 26 = 0$, i.e. $r = 26$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$r = 3$': coefficient is $3 - 26 = -23 ≠ 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice B — '$r = 7$': coefficient is $7 - 26 = -19 ≠ 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice C — '$r = 22$': coefficient is $22 - 26 = -4 ≠ 0$, so one solution exists. ✗
  • Choice D — '$r = 26$': coefficient is $26 - 26 = 0$, giving $0 = 22$ — no solution. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
